parentQuestion: A tissue consists of groups of similar cells that have a common function.

scaffolds.q: Cells are the basic structural units of living organisms.
scaffolds.explain: Cells are the smallest units capable of carrying out life processes. All living things — every single one — are made of one or more cells. Below the cellular level? You have molecules, atoms, chemistry. NOT life. Carry this forward:.

scaffolds.q: Similar cells often group together to perform specific tasks.
scaffolds.explain: Just as workers with similar skills are grouped into departments, similar cells group together into tissues to perform specific functions more efficiently. Specialization plus cooperation equals POWER. Dive into this:.
